mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 7 Series 750i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 750i engine typically costs £4,500–£9,500 supply only, with the N63 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on the specialist. A blown engine replacement can cost between £9,000 and £13,000. A used N62B48B engine costs on average around £4,489. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 7 Series.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 7 Series 750i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which generation you have. The E38 M73 V12 is a classic — smooth and robust, but ageing. The N62 (E65/E66) suffers from valve stem seal and cooling system issues. The early N63 (F01/F02 pre-2012) is the high-risk choice — timing chain stretch, excessive oil consumption, and injector failure are common. Later N63TU and N63TU2/TU3 versions (2012+) are significantly improved. All N63s require obsessive maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 7 Series 750i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N63 timing chain stretch and guide failure (pre-2012 F01/F02) — plastic guides break, sending debris through the engine. Excessive oil consumption from worn valve stem seals across N62 and N63 variants. Fuel injector failure on early N63 engines. N62 cooling system failures. The N63TU2/TU3 (G11/G12) is significantly improved.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 750i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G12 worth £20,000–£35,000, a £7,000–£9,500 recon is 20–40% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a tired E65 worth £4,000–£7,000, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. With labour times often exceeding 30 hours for a full replacement, many cars are written off rather than repaired.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 7 Series 750i engine code?
Can I repair the N63 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing chain and guides must be replaced if stretched or worn. A preventative replacement costs £3,000–£5,000 (engine-out job). A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £6,000–£9,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The 750i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: 5.4L V12 — 326 bhp; smooth and robust; timing chain guides and coolant system are the primary watch items
4.8L V8 — 367 bhp; valve stem seals and cooling system are known weaknesses
4.4L twin-turbo V8 — 407 bhp, 600 Nm; early N63 — excessive oil consumption, timing chain stretch, injector failure
4.4L twin-turbo V8 — 450 bhp, 650 Nm; first major revision (N63TU) — some improvements but oil consumption remains common
4.4L twin-turbo V8 — up to 530 bhp in LCI; N63TU2/TU3 — most refined N63; chain issues largely resolved but still high-maintenance

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current 750i replacement costs. The N63 variants typically sit at the higher end of the range. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 750i engine typically takes 16–22 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 750i? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2015 G11/G12 with the N63TU2/TU3 (most refined N63 versions)
* Post-2012 F01/F02 with N63TU (improved over early N63)
* Documented timing chain history on any N63-engined car
* Valve stem seals replaced on N62 or N63 (if high-mileage)
* Full service history with frequent oil changes (critical for N63 longevity)
* E38 V12 with documented maintenance (classic appeal; parts availability is a consideration)
avoidIf: * Pre-2012 N63 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — the N63 is unforgiving of neglect
* Excessive oil consumption or blue smoke — valve stem seals likely failing
* Overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system issues may have caused damage
* Any N63 with a history of catastrophic failure — walk away

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 750i is BMW's flagship V8 (and historically V12) 7 Series — offering effortless luxury performance. The E38 V12 is a classic with genuine character but ageing maintenance demands. The N62 (E65/E66) is a capable V8 with valve stem seal and cooling system vulnerabilities. The early N63 (F01/F02 pre-2012) is the high-risk choice — timing chain stretch, oil consumption, and injector failure are common. Later N63TU and N63TU2/TU3 versions (2012+) are significantly improved, with the G11/G12 LCI (2019+) offering the best reliability of the N63 family. However, all N63s require obsessive maintenance — frequent oil changes and careful monitoring. With typical 7 Series values ranging from £5,000 (E65) to £30,000+ (G12), an engine replacement is a major financial commitment. A post-2015 G11/G12 with documented history is the safest choice.

cta.label: → Compare 750i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 7 Series 750i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (N63 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (M73, N62, N63O0/O1, N63TU2/TU3) and oil-change frequency
* Oil Consumption — Ask about oil top-up frequency; excessive consumption may indicate valve stem seal wear
* Blue Smoke — Check on startup; any blue smoke indicates oil burning
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss may indicate cooling system leaks
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or hesitation?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate injector or timing problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a BMW 7 Series 750i engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned 750i engine typically costs £4,500–£9,500 supply only, with the N63 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 depending on the specialist. A blown engine replacement can cost between £9,000 and £13,000. A used N62B48B engine costs on average around £4,489. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 7 Series.
items.value: 2. Is the BMW 7 Series 750i engine reliable?

items.label: It depends entirely on which generation you have. The E38 M73 V12 is a classic — smooth and robust, but ageing. The N62 (E65/E66) suffers from valve stem seal and cooling system issues. The early N63 (F01/F02 pre-2012) is the high-risk choice — timing chain stretch, excessive oil consumption, and injector failure are common. Later N63TU and N63TU2/TU3 versions (2012+) are significantly improved. All N63s require obsessive maintenance.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the BMW 7 Series 750i?

items.label: N63 timing chain stretch and guide failure (pre-2012 F01/F02) — plastic guides break, sending debris through the engine. Excessive oil consumption from worn valve stem seals across N62 and N63 variants. Fuel injector failure on early N63 engines. N62 cooling system failures. The N63TU2/TU3 (G11/G12) is significantly improved.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a 750i engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G12 worth £20,000–£35,000, a £7,000–£9,500 recon is 20–40% of the car's value — often worth it. Against a tired E65 worth £4,000–£7,000, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. With labour times often exceeding 30 hours for a full replacement, many cars are written off rather than repaired.
items.value: 5. How do I find my BMW 7 Series 750i engine code?

items.label: E38 (1994–2001): M73B54. E65/E66 (2001–2009): N62B48B. F01/F02 (2009–2012): N63B44O0. F01/F02 LCI (2012–2015): N63B44O1. G11/G12 (2015–2022): N63B44O2/N63TU2 or N63B44T3 (LCI). Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the N63 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

items.label: The timing chain and guides must be replaced if stretched or worn. A preventative replacement costs £3,000–£5,000 (engine-out job). A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £6,000–£9,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
items.value: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine condition, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited-mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
